steps.title: Recognize the complex list structure
steps.content: The sentence lists three deserts. Each item contains an internal comma (for instance, “the Syrian Desert, ranked ninth in size”). This is the key signal: when list items contain commas within them, semicolons must separate the items instead.

steps.title: Apply the semicolon rule
steps.content:
Choice B correctly uses semicolons to separate the three desert items with their rankings: the Syrian Desert, ranked ninth in size; the Kyzylkum Desert, ranked sixteenth; and the Ferlo Desert, ranked thirty-second.

The other choices either lack the necessary punctuation (A), place semicolons incorrectly and break the series structure (C), or inconsistently mix punctuation (D).

That matches choice B — B is correct.
